strengths: Nearly 20 years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, desktop, embedded and high-throughput backend systems — comfortably exceeding the 15-year requirement

Current AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) plus founder experience with GISCollective demonstrates the builder fluency and entrepreneurial drive the role prefers
Proven track record of inspiring and educating others — conference talks (FOSS4G), multi-day courses, workshops and dojos — matching the role's mandate to share insights internally and externally
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, only recently moved into AI-focused work
Career titles top out at Staff/Senior; no formal Director-level or organization-wide strategic leadership role on record
Cloud experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) is present but not positioned as deep platform-strategy expertise at the scale the role implies

key_factors.factor: Depth and breadth of engineering experience (19+ years)
Active AI/agentic tooling work and entrepreneurial founder background
Demonstrated thought leadership through talks, workshops and open source

response.justification: The candidate brings nearly two decades of versatile, hands-on engineering — from low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second to full-stack product work — comfortably clearing the experience bar. His current role building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure gives him timely, practical AI credibility, while founding and operating an open-source geo-data platform shows the entrepreneurial, builder mindset the role explicitly prefers. A long history of teaching, speaking and community leadership maps directly to the responsibility of inspiring teams through disruptive work, making him a strong honest case for advancement despite lacking a formal AI degree or prior director title.
